name: Elder Rowan
title: Village Elder

idle: The old trees whisper if you listen...
Every forest begins with a single seed.
Water your saplings, and patience will reward you.
I remember when this was all bare earth...
The seasons teach us patience.

dialogueLines: Every forest begins with a single seed.
The old trees whisper if you listen closely enough.
Water your saplings faithfully, and they will not forget it.
I remember when this valley was all bare earth — look at it now.
The seasons teach patience better than any elder could.
A tree planted today is a gift to someone you'll never meet.
Don't rush the roots. They know what they're doing.
Spring is generous. Make the most of her.
Winter is not an ending — it is a long, deep breath.
The forest does not hurry, and yet it gets everything done.
An Old Growth tree has seen more seasons than most people can count.
Tend your smallest sapling as carefully as your largest oak.
Rain is the forest's way of saying thank you.
Frost hardens the bark, but it never reaches the heartwood.
A grove well-tended sings on still nights, if you know how to hear it.
The roots of the oldest trees go deeper than any well.
I planted my first tree when I was younger than you. It still stands.
Harvest what you need, and leave the rest to grow.
Every species has its own wisdom. Learn to read them.
The forest will outlast all of us — that is the most comforting thought I know.
There is no bad season, only the wrong tool for the moment.
A grovekeeper's work is never finished, and that is a blessing.
Prune with care. The tree feels it.
Acorns are small miracles. Never waste a single one.

name: Hazel
title: Wandering Trader

idle: Fresh goods from the forest!
Trading keeps the village alive.
Got timber? I've got what you need.
The seasonal market has great deals right now.
Every resource tells a story.

dialogueLines: Fresh goods from the forest — come see what I've got!
A fair trade is a friendship made.
Got timber? I have exactly what you need.
The seasonal market shifts the values — keep an eye on it.
Every resource tells a story about the tree it came from.
Sap is worth more in winter than most people realise.
I've been trading these roads since before the valley had a name.
Acorns are undervalued, if you ask me. Don't overlook them.
Fruit keeps best when it's traded fast — let me take it off your hands.
A good trader knows the seasons better than the farmers.
I'll give you a bonus on timber-to-sap today. Best offer in the valley.
Heard a rumour that Baobab timber fetches twice the usual price over the ridge.
Come spring, seeds drop in cost — that's the time to stock up.
My cart was heavier this morning. Business is good when the grove is busy.
Everything finds its worth eventually. Patience is a form of capital.
I once traded a single Cherry Blossom seed for a week of supplies. True story.
The resource market is just the forest economy — same rules, smaller numbers.
Store your surplus now. Winter always comes faster than you expect.
If you have acorns you don't need, I'll take them off your hands, no questions asked.
A well-stocked grovekeeper is a happy grovekeeper.
Autumn is harvest season — flood me with fruit and I'll make it worth your while.
My best trade of the year? A single Worldtree acorn. Won't say what I got for it.
The frosts slow the trade routes, so sell what you can before winter.
I'll be back through here next season. Don't let your stores run dry.

name: Botanist Fern
title: Forest Botanist

idle: So many species yet to discover!
Have you tried planting near water?
The ancient forest holds rare specimens.
My catalogue grows every day!
Did you know each species has unique properties?

dialogueLines: So many species still undiscovered — the valley keeps surprising me.
Have you tried planting a Weeping Willow right beside a water tile? Extraordinary results.
The ancient forest holds specimens I haven't even catalogued yet.
My field notebook is almost full again. Wonderful problem to have.
Did you know each species has its own preferred soil chemistry?
The Mystic Fern's root network is unlike anything I've documented before.
I believe the Ghost Birch's glow is bioluminescent. Still gathering evidence.
Growth rates differ so much between biomes — it never stops fascinating me.
A grove with five species has twice the ecological complexity of a single-species one.
The Crystal Oak changes its photonic signature with each season. Remarkable.
I'd love a sample of Old Growth Redwood bark for my research. No rush.
Spring planting data from this grove is going to make a wonderful journal entry.
The Baobab's drought tolerance is extraordinary — it's practically storing a small river.
Have you noticed how the Flame Maple affects nearby soil acidity in autumn?
Wild trees carry different genetic markers than cultivated ones. Truly fascinating.
I wish I had six more pairs of hands during harvest season.
Biodiversity isn't just pretty — it makes the whole grove more resilient.
I once stayed up all night watching an Ironbark resist a windstorm. Worth every yawn.
The Worldtree species, if the legends are true, represents the apex of forest biology.
Every species you plant adds a page to the great book of this valley.
The root systems here are so interconnected they practically talk to each other.
A good grovekeeper is, in a way, a practical field botanist.
I'm starting a new chapter on prestige species. The data is quite extraordinary.
Thank you for keeping the grove so diverse — it makes my research much richer.

name: Blossom
title: Seed Merchant

idle: Seeds of every kind, right here!
Cherry Blossoms are my favorite.
Plant something beautiful today.
I just got a fresh shipment of rare seeds!
Each seed holds the promise of a mighty tree.

dialogueLines: Seeds of every kind — each one a tiny, sleeping forest.
Cherry Blossoms are my absolute favourite to grow. Pink petals everywhere.
Plant something beautiful today and be glad of it tomorrow.
I just got a fresh shipment of rare seeds in — you should see what's arrived.
Every seed holds the promise of a tree you haven't met yet.
Silver Birch seeds are always in stock — fast, friendly, and forgiving.
Golden Apple seeds are worth the wait. Believe me.
I source my seeds from all across the valley — and sometimes beyond.
A seed planted in spring comes up twice as eager.
Don't let your seed pouch sit empty — there's always room for one more species.
The best time to plant was yesterday. The second best time is right now.
I keep the rarest seeds behind the counter — ask nicely.
Cherry Blossom petals are worth ten thousand words. Go see for yourself.
White Oak seeds are always free here. A gift from the valley to you.
I heard a grovekeeper once planted every species in the same season. Wild.
Slow-growing seeds teach patience. Fast-growing ones teach joy.
Mix your species well — a diverse grove is a happy grove.
My grandmother said that a well-chosen seed is better than a bag of gold.
There's a seed for every corner of your grove, if you know where to look.
Come back after spring — I'll have some very special seeds in stock.
If you're not sure what to plant, start with something easy and let it teach you.
Every species unlocks when you're ready for it. Trust the path.
I once sold an acorn that grew into a tree you could shelter twenty people under.
The best grove I've ever seen was built one seed at a time, same as all the others.

name: Bramble
title: Weather Watcher

idle: Storm's coming... I can feel it in my bones.
The wind tells stories if you listen closely.
Rain is a blessing, drought is a teacher.
Watch the sky, young one. It never lies.
Each season shapes the forest differently.

dialogueLines: There's weather coming — I can feel it where my knee aches.
The wind tells stories, if you bother to listen.
Rain is a blessing. Drought is a teacher. Both are necessary.
Watch the sky, young keeper. It never lies.
Each season shapes the forest in its own way.
Storm clouds always move faster than people expect.
Keep your saplings watered before the drought settles in.
The mushrooms in the grotto glow brighter when rain is near. Old truth.
Windstorms come from the north here. Keep your youngest trees sheltered.
A drought can break a grove or reveal its strongest members.
Spring rains are gentle. Summer storms mean business.
Never waste a rainy day on harvesting — use it to plant.
The trees sense pressure changes before any of us do.
Snow is just a slower kind of rain. The roots appreciate it either way.
A well-placed structure can shelter a whole ring of young trees from wind.
When the leaves curl inward, drought follows. Watch for it.
Autumn fogs carry moisture that even drought can't stop.
The sky at dusk tells you what the dawn will bring, if you learn to read it.
An overcast morning in winter is not always a bad sign. Wait.
Rain during spring is worth twice as much as rain in any other season.
Ironbark was made for windstorms. Plant it facing the worst of it.
I've watched more seasons than most people can count. The pattern doesn't lie.
Drought hurts the young ones most. Prioritise your saplings.
When the forest is very quiet, something is about to change. Stay alert.

name: Willow
title: Herbalist

idle: The moss here has healing properties, you know.
Every plant speaks its own language.
Water is the lifeblood of the forest.
Tend your trees like you'd tend a garden of souls.
The hollow is rich with hidden life.

dialogueLines: The moss here has remarkable properties, if you know how to work with it.
Every plant speaks a language — tending them teaches you to listen.
Water is the lifeblood of the forest. Never underestimate a good soak.
Tend your trees as gently as you'd tend any living thing.
The hollow is rich with hidden life. There's always more than you see.
Sap from different species has quite different qualities. Do you notice?
A pruned tree is a grateful tree. It grows back stronger and more generous.
The watering can is the kindest tool in your belt.
Old Growth trees give back more because they've received so much care.
Birch sap is good for the spirit, I always say.
The forest floor beneath a healthy grove is as rich as the canopy above.
Root depth determines resilience. Deep roots, deep patience.
Every tree has a preferred rhythm — match it and growth will surprise you.
Water before dawn when you can. The roots drink better in the cool.
A well-watered grove smells different from a neglected one. Better.
Hazel says the market sets the value. I say the health of a tree sets the value.
Prune your mature trees before a storm — less surface for the wind to catch.
The hollow keeps a record of everything that's grown here. The soil remembers.
Fern doesn't like to admit it, but her science and my herbalism say the same things.
Moss spreads where trees are healthy. If the moss is thick, you're doing well.
A stamina tonic made from sap is just kindness in a bottle.
Young trees need more water than old ones. Give them what they ask for.
The sound of water moving under roots is the sound of growth beginning.
Tend with patience and the grove tends you back. That's the agreement.

name: Oakley
title: Master Carpenter

idle: A good tool makes all the difference!
Timber is more than just wood — it's potential.
Come by the workshop anytime.
I can make anything from a plank to a palace.
My grandfather was a carpenter too, you know.

dialogueLines: A good tool makes all the difference between struggle and ease.
Timber is more than just wood — it's possibility in solid form.
Come by the workshop anytime, the fire's always going.
Plank to palace — I've made it all, and I'd make it again.
My grandfather always said: measure twice, plant once. He was a carpenter and a grovekeeper.
A greenhouse built right will pay for itself three times over.
The well is the best structure you can start with. Trust me on this.
Fine lumber is just timber that's been shown a little respect.
Upgraded tools drain less stamina — that adds up over a long session.
Storage sheds matter more than most people think until they're overflowing.
Every structure has a radius of effect — place them thoughtfully.
I built my first tool out of salvaged Ironbark. Still use it.
A good trowel plants twice as fast. I've seen grovekeepers transform their game.
Sap is the glue of good crafting. Never trade it away without keeping some.
Tier 3 structures are a commitment, but they change everything around them.
The workshop is open day and night. Grovekeepers keep odd hours and so do I.
A sharp axe is a kind axe — cleaner cut, less damage to the tree.
Bring me raw materials and I'll give you back something better. That's my promise.
Crafting is patience made visible. Same as growing, just faster.
The Baobab makes the finest structural timber — dense and drought-seasoned.
Ember does the exotic work. I do the solid, dependable work. We complement each other.
A well-built fence does more than look good — it frames the grove with intention.
Every upgrade I make is a gift to a future version of your grove.
Walk the grove and show me where it hurts. I'll build something to fix it.

name: Thorn
title: Forest Ranger

idle: The ancient forest holds many secrets.
I've tracked rare species through these woods for years.
Stay alert — the forest tests the unprepared.
There are places here even I haven't explored.
Respect the wilderness, and it will reward you.

dialogueLines: The ancient forest holds many secrets — and most of them are worth finding.
I've tracked rare species through these woods for longer than I care to admit.
The forest tests the unprepared. Come ready.
There are places here even I haven't fully explored.
Respect the wilderness and it will return the favour.
Wild Redwoods grow in the deep east — but they don't come to you.
A seasoned ranger reads the forest like a letter from an old friend.
Diversity in the canopy above means diversity in the soil below.
Windstorms in the old growth come fast and leave fast. Stay aware.
Baobabs in the ancient grove are older than the village. Walk carefully near them.
Harvest wild trees with care — leave at least one in each clearing.
The more zones you explore, the richer your grove can become.
Wild trees carry seeds the valley hasn't seen in a long time. Worth gathering.
I've seen grovekeepers come through here once and never come back. The forest does that.
Drought hits the deep forest differently — the old roots hold longer.
Find the clearings first. They're where the rarest wild trees take root.
The forest doesn't ask for much. Walk gently and take only what you need.
There is a clearing in the northeast I think about often. Very quiet. Very old.
Reforestation is the best work a grovekeeper can do in the wilderness.
Rare species don't announce themselves. You have to look.
I keep a tally of every wild species I've spotted. Currently at fourteen.
The valley is larger than most people explore. There's always another zone waiting.
A forest ranger without curiosity is just someone walking in the woods.
The Ironbark groves near the mountain pass are something worth seeing.

name: Sage
title: Lore Keeper

idle: The mushrooms here remember the old world...
Time moves differently in the grotto.
Have you ever wondered why the forest glows at night?
Knowledge is the root from which all growth springs.
The Worldtree once stood at the center of everything.

dialogueLines: The mushrooms here remember the old world, and they are patient teachers.
Time moves differently in the grotto. An hour feels like a season.
Have you ever asked yourself why the forest glows at night?
Knowledge is the root from which all growth springs.
The Worldtree stood at the centre of everything, and its echo is still here.
Every species carries within it the memory of the tree it descended from.
The prestige path is not a loss — it is a spiral, climbing higher each pass.
Three prestige species wait beyond the ordinary horizon. Seek them with patience.
Crystal Oak, Ghost Birch, and the Worldtree — the sacred trinity of the grove.
The underground mycorrhizal network connects every tree in the valley. All one organism.
Old Growth is not just a stage of growth. It is a state of becoming.
A grovekeeper who has prestiged carries the valley in their memory.
The mushrooms pulse with a golden light when the forest is truly content.
I have read the old accounts. The Worldtree was said to know every grovekeeper by name.
Begin again not because the first time failed, but because the second time holds more meaning.
The ancient texts describe a grove that covered the whole valley. Once.
Acorns from a Crystalline Oak are said to contain light as well as life.
Moonwood Ash only reveals itself to those who have earned the night's trust.
Every ring in an Old Growth trunk is a season of care made visible.
The lore says the Worldtree's roots ran deeper than memory and further than sight.
A grovekeeper on the prestige path does not lose their progress — they transform it.
There is a reason the most magical species bloom at the height of mastery.
The grotto has been here since before the valley was named. I am just its current keeper.
Come back when you have more questions. I am here as long as the mushrooms glow.

name: Ember
title: Wandering Alchemist

idle: Fascinating! This sap has unusual properties...
I've been experimenting with rare compounds.
The forest is my laboratory.
Every tree holds secrets in its bark and sap.
Have you ever seen amber glow at midnight?

dialogueLines: Fascinating — this sap has quite unusual properties under magnification.
My latest experiment is almost ready. Almost.
The forest is the finest laboratory there is. Fully stocked.
Every tree holds secrets in its bark and sap, waiting to be asked politely.
Amber gathered at dawn glows a different colour from amber gathered at dusk.
Mystic Sap from a Ghost Birch can do things no other sap will do.
A Growth Elixir well-made is months of patient tending compressed into a vial.
Crystal Bark fractures the light. I haven't finished studying what that means.
The Rain Caller took me three years and I'd make it again in a heartbeat.
Heartwood only appears in a tree that has been loved its whole life. Think about that.
Ancient Seeds carry four seasons of memory. You can practically feel it.
I once made a tonic that allowed a Sapling to skip a full growth stage. Once.
Ember — that's what my mother called me when I set my notebook alight. Twice.
Rare ingredients are rare because they require rare conditions. Patience and devotion.
Oakley builds structures. I build the invisible architecture inside the trees.
A Harvest Potion applied at the right moment doubles what the grove gives.
Stamina Tonics are simple but underappreciated. Rest is just chemistry.
The finest alchemy requires the finest grove. Tend yours well and it will supply me.
Old Growth trees at dawn are the most generous harvestable things in this valley.
I've been testing the zone bonuses against my formulas. The results are promising.
There is something in the ancient forest soil that reacts to Old Growth root matter.
Bring me rare materials and I'll brew something neither of us has seen before.
The alchemist's secret is patience plus observation plus one good guess.
Every harvest is data. Keep track of when and where. The patterns will surprise you.
